description.p: Boo is a local speech-to-text overlay that captures audio from your microphone and transcribes it on-device using whisper.cpp. Press Ctrl+Shift+Space (after approving the shortcut on first launch) or click the Record button, speak, and the transcript is copied to the clipboard and pasted into the focused app.
Everything runs locally — no audio leaves your machine.
Preview on Linux: recording, transcription and both portal permissions are verified, but Boo has not yet been run on a real GNOME or KDE desktop. Bug reports are welcome.
Correctness, security, and a project-wide quality pass.

ul.li: Boo now finds any whisper model, not only the one it recommends — previously dropping a different model where the docs said to put it was reported as "no model installed".
The RemoteDesktop restore token is written owner-only (0600); it was world-readable.
Fewer permission prompts: macOS asks for Accessibility only when it first needs it, and Linux no longer re-runs the shortcut approval dialog on every launch.

description.p: Fixes found by running Boo on a real GNOME desktop, and by testing it the way a new user would.

ul.li: Boo now tells you when your desktop has no global-shortcuts portal. GNOME only gained one in version 48, so on GNOME 46 (Ubuntu 24.04 LTS) the hotkey cannot work for any app — previously Boo failed silently. Auto-paste is unaffected, so Boo stays usable from the Record button.

Non-English dictation works. The language was hardcoded to English, which silently translated other languages instead of transcribing them; set BOO_LANG (a language code, or "auto") when using a multilingual model.
A single recording is now capped at 10 minutes and stops automatically, transcribing what it captured. Previously the buffer grew without limit and could freeze the app for minutes inside whisper.
The menu bar now shows when Boo is transcribing, rather than snapping straight back to idle while whisper is still working.
Fixed a memory leak, a data race, and a latent use-after-free in the audio backends.

description.p: First Linux preview, and the first build for Intel Macs.

ul.li: Linux ships as a Flatpak. Audio capture, transcription, the global hotkey and auto-paste are all verified; Boo has not yet been run on a real GNOME or KDE desktop.
macOS now builds for Intel as well as Apple Silicon.
On-device speech-to-text with whisper.cpp — audio never leaves the machine.
Global hotkey (Ctrl+Shift+Space) via the GlobalShortcuts portal.
Transcripts are copied to the clipboard and pasted into the focused app via the RemoteDesktop portal.
